interactions.summary: Jiang interprets the Calypso passage as evidence of depression and asks why Odysseus has become broken. He then frames Calypso's immortality offer as a temptation Odysseus refuses because he needs home, and sets up the banquet where a bard's Trojan War song will reopen the wound.
A student reads Odyssey passages describing Hermes' command reaching Calypso, Odysseus weeping on the headland and beach, and the bard's title for the song about Odysseus and Achilles.

speaker_notes.note: SPEAKER_00 is quoted Odyssey reading. Jiang's prompts such as 'Do you want to go in?' and 'can you read' are classroom management, not substantive questions.

claims.claim: Odysseus' stillness, constant weeping, and fixation on the past are read as clear signs of depression and PTSD.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim stated in the March 4, 2026 lecture.

claims.claim: Calypso offers Odysseus immortal captivity, but Jiang reads Odysseus as needing to refuse immortality because home is necessary for repair.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim stated in the March 4, 2026 lecture.

claims.claim: The banquet tradition matters because the bard's song of great deeds will force Odysseus to hear a heroic version of the Trojan War while hiding his identity.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim stated in the March 4, 2026 lecture.

claims.claim: The quoted Odyssey passages present Odysseus as an unwilling lover by night and a grieving, homeward-looking man by day.
claims.temporal_scope: Quoted Homer passage read in the March 4, 2026 lecture.

signature_moments.moment: The hero is motionless on the headland, eyes never dry, his life flowing away through tears.
signature_moments.source_phrase: his sweet life flowing away with the tears
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This image makes the broken Odyssey Odysseus visible before Jiang explains the wound.

signature_moments.moment: Immortality is not enough: Odysseus can be offered forever and still need home.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I can make you immortal... but I need to go home
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It turns homecoming into a deeper need than pleasure, safety, or eternal life.

signature_moments.moment: A banquet song becomes the therapeutic trap: public heroic memory will expose private trauma.
signature_moments.source_phrase: have a bard sing a great story
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It sets up poetry as the mechanism that forces Odysseus to remember.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The banquet practice of singing great deeds, which in Jiang's reading reactivates Odysseus' traumatic memory of Troy.
The temptation to remain in immortal captivity instead of returning home and confronting pain.

chronology_notes.note: The March 4, 2026 lecture moves from Calypso releasing Odysseus to the later banquet where the Trojan War is sung before him.

uncertainty_notes.note: ASR says 'PDST'; extraction treats Jiang's intended term as PTSD because the surrounding lecture repeatedly names post-traumatic stress.
